channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Alabama carries out nation’s third nitrogen gas execution on a man for a hitchhiker's killing
item.description: ATMORE, Ala. (AP) — An Alabama man convicted in the 1994 killing of a hitchhiker cursed at the prison warden and made obscene gestures with his hands shortly before he was put to death Thursday evening in the nation’s third execution using nitrogen gas.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Gaetz withdrawal from AG nomination is among quickest in history
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) withdrew his nomination as President-elect Trump’s attorney general on Thursday, putting an end to a chaotic several days on Capitol Hill and making him one of the quickest in history to withdraw from Cabinet nominations.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: What's next for Matt Gaetz: 5 possibilities
item.description: Here are five possibilities for what could come next for Gaetz following his announcement that he is taking his name out of the running to be attorney general in the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: 2 House Republicans seek to stop IVF expansion in defense bill
item.description: The topic came to the forefront after the Supreme Court overturned Roe v. Wade, spurred by social conservatives’ belief that life begins at conception.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: South Dakota Republican introduces bill to eliminate Department of Education
item.description: The proposal would get rid of the federal agency and move all critical programs to other departments.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Police warn of sophisticated holiday scam artists targeting consumers
item.description: He said any time shoppers use their credit cards or personal information, they should go in with their eyes open.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, NewsNation

item.title: Police report reveals assault allegations against Hegseth, Trump's pick for defense secretary
item.description: SANTA CRUZ, Calif. (AP) — A woman told police that she was sexually assaulted in 2017 by Pete Hegseth after he took her phone, blocked the door to a California hotel room and refused to let her leave, according to a detailed investigative report made public late Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA MENDOZA, BRIAN SLODYSKO and JULIET LINDERMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What to know about a storm bringing high winds, heavy rain, snow to California and Pacific Northwest
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — One of the strongest storms on the West Coast in decades knocked out power for thousands of people, unleashed strong winds that toppled trees and left two dead in Washington before making its way through Oregon to Northern California where on Thursday it dropped heavy snow and r
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A kayaker faked his own death and fled overseas. Now he's telling how he did it
item.description: GREEN LAKE, Wis. (AP) — A Wisconsin man who faked his own drowning this summer so he could abandon his wife and three children has been communicating with authorities daily from Eastern Europe, even telling them how he did it, but has not committed to returning home, a sheriff said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What happened to JonBenét Ramsey? New documentary set to premiere this month
item.description: The death of JonBenét Ramsey remains unsolved and has been the subject of a number of documentaries and books.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Jarpe, NewsNation

item.title: Attorneys want the US Supreme Court to say Mississippi's felony voting ban is cruel and unusual
item.description: JACKSON, Miss. (AP) — The U.S. Supreme Court should overturn Mississippi's Jim Crow-era practice of removing voting rights from people convicted of certain felonies, including nonviolent crimes such as forgery and timber theft, attorneys say in new court papers.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kit Kats to come in new shape for the first time ever in America
item.description: The Hershey Company has debuted Kit Kats in the shape of something other than a traditional Kit Kat "finger.
item.dc:creator: Hayden Sherry, KRON4

item.title: Weird laws in Massachusetts including a $20 fine, possible jail time for frightening a pigeon
item.description: In Massachusetts, be cautious before dyeing a chick, defacing a milk carton or scaring a pigeon, as these acts are all illegal in the state.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Meghan Markle's bad week as Prince Harry outshines his wife
item.description: Across the pond in America, former British royal, Prince Harry, is making inroads to make himself likable again… and it’s working.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: Wisconsin man who faked his own kayaking death fled the country and won't come home, officials say
item.description: Borgwardt, 45, was reported missing on Aug. 12 after he failed to return home from kayaking on Big Green Lake, the Green Lake County Sheriff’s Office said at the time.
item.dc:creator: Ben Newhouse, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pathologist disputes finding that Marine veteran's chokehold caused subway rider's death
item.description: A forensic pathologist disputes a New York City medical examiner's determination that former Marine Daniel Penny killed another man by placing him in a chokehold on a subway train last year.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Peltz, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What foods you can -- and can't -- take on a plane, according to TSA
item.description: Before you pack your bags to bring a family-favorite food to the table, it's important to know what is allowed through airport security - and what isn't.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: US towns plunge into debates about fluoride in water
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — For about 50 years, adding cavity-preventing fluoride to drinking water was a popular public health measure in Yorktown, a leafy town north of New York City. But in September, the town’s supervisor used his emergency powers to stop the practice.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Connecticut manhunt underway after suspect allegedly kills mother, infant in drive-by shooting
item.description: A multi-agency manhunt is underway for Lance Morales, a 23-year-old accused of killing a young mother and her 4-month-old child in a drive-by shooting in Hartford, Conn.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Georgia's Brian Kemp picked to lead Republican Governors Association
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp will lead the Republican Governors Association for the next year, the group announced Wednesday, just weeks after the party secured governorships in eight out of 11 state races up for grabs in the election earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE KRAMON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Wisconsin man who faked his own kayaking death fled the country and won't come home, officials say
item.description: Borgwardt, 45, was reported missing on Aug. 12 after he failed to return home from kayaking on Big Green Lake, the Green Lake County Sheriff’s Office said at the time.
item.dc:creator: Ben Newhouse, NewsNation

item.title: Whooping cough cases have quintupled. These states have it worst
item.description: Pertussis – a disease commonly referred to as whooping cough – is spreading quickly, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention warns. The U.S. has seen five times more cases in 2024 than we did at this time last year.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: DOJ asks judge to order Google to sell Chrome
item.description: The DOJ’s proposal would require Google to divest from Chrome and prohibit the search giant from owning another browser for five years.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, Fox 5

item.title: Tennessee man convicted for Jan. 6 riot found guilty of plotting to kill FBI agents who investigated him
item.description: A Tennessee man was found guilty of plotting to have the FBI agents who investigated him for his role in the Jan. 6 riot at the U.S. Capitol murdered.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NYC tourist stabbed in swanky area after 3 killed in random attack as DA slams Trump
item.description: A 55-year-old tourist was slashed in his face on the Upper West Side in New York City in another unprovoked street stabbing, police confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florist gets death threats after refusing to serve Trump voters
item.description: The owner posted on social media that the business is now temporarily closed after receiving several death threats. She also made all of her social media accounts private.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Knox, Fox 5

item.title: Bryan Kohberger can face death penalty if convicted in Idaho college slayings
item.description: Bryan Kohberger, the man accused of killing four University of Idaho students in 2022, can face the death penalty, a judge ruled Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Not allowed: AMC issues strict warning to 'Wicked' fans ahead of film's premiere
item.description: Fans eager to belt out hits like "Defying Gravity" and "Popular" in theaters during the highly anticipated "Wicked" movie musical will need to hold off — for now.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Yakubovsky, KRON4

item.title: Man wins $650K on lottery ticket using numbers tied to late wife
item.description: A Chicago man won a $650,000 Lucky Day Lotto jackpot prize after playing numbers related to his late wife.
item.dc:creator: Marisa Rodriguez, KRON4

item.title: Las Vegas man facing eviction tried to kill puppies, police say
item.description: A man allegedly tried to kill several puppies by covering them in cleaning products and placing them in a trash bag in a dumpster because he was getting evicted, according to police documents.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, Fox 5

item.title: California governor will not make clemency decision for Menendez brothers until new DA reviews case
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California Gov. Gavin Newsom said he won't make a clemency decision on the murder convictions of Erik and Lyle Menendez until newly elected Los Angeles County District Attorney Nathan Hochman reviews the nearly 35-year-old case.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Rare coin found in piece of furniture sells for $2.52M at auction
item.description: A rare 17th Century coin sold at auction for a record-breaking $2.52 million eight years after it was found in an old cabinet.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: GOP senators reject replacing FBI background checks for Trump nominees
item.description: Senate Republicans are rejecting a proposal floated by some advisers to President-elect Trump to take the job of conducting background checks for high-level nominees away from the FBI and give it to private investigators.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: When Trump left office, students were struggling because of the pandemic. They still are
item.description: When President-elect Donald Trump left office in early 2021, the pandemic was at its height. Now he returns as American children and schools are struggling to overcome its lingering impact.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Mom's donated kidney to Oregon son turns 100
item.description: Ken Reese remembers how selfless his mother, Doris, was. When he was a high school student 49 years ago, his mom gave him a new lease on life by donating one of her kidneys to him.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Dinh, Fox 5

item.title: Here are the benefits all US presidents get when they retire
item.description: When a president leaves office, the checks and other benefits continue. Here’s a look at some of those perks, and how they came about.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, Fox 5

item.title: Beyoncé performing at halftime of NFL Christmas Day game on Netflix
item.description: Beyoncé is coming to your home on Christmas — provided you have Netflix and are tuning in to the Baltimore Ravens-Houston Texans game.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
